title: SK Telecom Pours $91.5B Into AI Power - TCR 07/05/26
description:
SK Telecom committed $91.5 billion to a 15-gigawatt AI data center fleet needing three million GPUs, National Grid Ventures took a $1.75 billion stake in a 2.67-gigawatt Texas gas plant wired to a Microsoft data center, and a Guardian investigation found £20 billion of OpenAI's touted £30 billion Stargate UK was hypothetical.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-july-5-2026/

0:00 The AI buildout gets poured across three continents
0:48 The AI squeeze reaches the jobs data
3:45 OpenAI's phantom Stargate UK
5:39 Alibaba walls off Claude Code
8:35 Regulators admit the rulebook trails AI
11:40 The Watchdog That Widened Its Own Error Bars
14:47 Synthesis and Closing

This episode covers the SK Telecom, National Grid, and NTT compute buildout, OpenAI's phantom Stargate UK, Alibaba banning Claude Code as Anthropic confirms a China-user-detection experiment, June's 57,000-job report against 2.85 million open listings, and regulators conceding the rulebook trails AI capability.

Three commitments landed in one news cycle, each measuring the intelligence buildout in physical units: SK Telecom's $91.5 billion for a 15-gigawatt fleet, National Grid Ventures' $1.75 billion stake in a Texas gas plant dedicated to a Microsoft data center, and NTT's prefab method claiming to halve hyperscale build time. Against that poured concrete, a Guardian investigation found OpenAI's £30 billion Stargate UK resting on thinner ground, with roughly £20 billion hypothetical and the flagship Newcastle site apparently never visited. Alibaba then walled off Claude Code as high-risk after Anthropic confirmed a China-usage detection experiment, even as model capability keeps diffusing into open weights within months of any frontier demonstration. June added just 57,000 jobs with AI the top-cited layoff reason, while 2.85 million active listings show demand concentrating around people who direct AI rather than substitute for it. And European supervisors plus the UK's AI Safety Institute conceded the rulemaking cycle no longer fits the pace it governs, publishing evidence that fixed-budget evaluations understate frontier capability.

- The Guardian - OpenAI's apparent failure to visit key Stargate UK site: https://www.theguardian.com/technology/2026/jul/04/openai-apparent-failure-visit-key-site-questions-stargate-uk-project
